mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day forward returns in >60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity constraints and weakening manufacturing demand, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter as earnings growth stalls.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >58% of late-cycle periods with declining INDPRO, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by >2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with stronger corporate revenue trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon as markets price in improved fundamentals.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following positive GDPC1 revisions, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.05 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, borrowing costs severely constrain corporate leverage and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in >55% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment indicates a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in >60% of observations, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over PAYEMS alone (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week as investors price in stagflation risks.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >57% of post-shock periods with declining INDPRO, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.
